rulings.Case Title: AMERICAN MARKETING COMPLEX, INC. VS. CARDIAC NETWORK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 28, 2011, line 5, DEFENDANT DAVID LEVY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 5TH-9TH CAUSES OF ACTION TO PLEAD FACTS WITH GREATER SPECIFICITY. REGARDING THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES WITH REASONABLE PARTICULARITY AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CALIFORNIA, INC. (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612, 619.) AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HOW AND WHY A FIDUCIARY DUTY IS OWED BY THIS DEFENDANT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA PASCUBELLO VS. URBAN STRATEGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Juana Pascubello'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Complete Consolidation Of Case Cgc-11-509678 Or In The Alternative, Motion For Partial Consolidation
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 28, 2011, line 16, Plaintiff Juana Pascubello'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Complete Consolidation Of Case Cgc-11-509678 Or In The Alternative, Motion For Partial Consolidation. DENIED. NO NEW LAW OR FACTS SUPPORT THE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. MOTION FOR PARTIAL CONSOLIDATION IS DENIED. THESE MATTERS ARE LIKELY TO PROCEED BEFORE ONE JUDGE COMMENCING OCTOBER 3RD. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUANA PASCUBELLO VS. URBAN STRATEGIES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Urban Strategies, Inc. Motion For A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 28, 2011, line 16, Defendant Urban Strategies, Inc.'s Motion For A Protective Order. GRANTED IN PART THAT SETS DATES FOR THE DEPOSITIONS. DENIED REGARDING REQUEST THAT EACH PARTY IS NOT AT EACH OF THE DEFENDANTS' DEPOSITIONS AT THE SAME TIME. PLAINTIFFS SHALL NOT ATTEND EACH OTHER'S DEPOSITIONS UNTIL THE PORTION OF THE DEPOSITION WITNESSING THE OTHER PLAINTIFF'S CASE. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W. THAYER VS. FOREST DENTAL PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 28, 2011, line 8, DEFENDANT HENRY SCHEIN, INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AND SERVED, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION: PLAINTIFF MUST ALLEGE ACTIONS BY DEFENDANT THAT SHOW UNFAIR METHODS OR PRACTICE. 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT ALLEGE ANY DUTY OWED BY DEFENDANTS TO PLAINTIFF, BREACH OF DUTY, OR IDENTIFY WHAT PIECE OF EQUIPMENT MALFUNCTIONED. 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION MUST STATE FACTS REGARDING INADEQUATE WARNINGS, WHAT THE PRODUCT WAS, OR WHETHER IT WAS BEING USED IN A FORESEEABLE MANNER. THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION DOES NOT STATE WITH PARTICULARITY WHAT ACTIVITIES ARE UNLAWFUL OR FRAUDULENT. (SEE KHOURY V. MALY'S OF CA. (1993) 14 CAL.APP.4TH 612.) AS TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, NO FACTS SHOW AN EXPRESS WARRANTY MADE TO PLAINTIFF, NOR WHAT IMPLIED WARRANTIES WERE BREACHED. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LAWRENCE M. ZOLOT VS. NORTHWESTERN MUTUAL LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, September 28, 2011, line 11, DEFENDANT COMMISSIONER OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. OVERRULED, 10 DAYS LEAVE TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES THE COMMISSIONER FAILED TO PERFORM THE MANDATORY DUTIES IMPOSED ON HIM TO REQUIRE COMPLIANCE WITH THE INSURANCE CODE. (PARAGRAPHS 62-63.) THE COMMISSIONER MUST REVIEW ANY INSURANCE POLICY SUBMITTED FOR APPROVAL IN CALIFORNIA AND MAKE A FINDING THAT THE POLICY COMPLIES WITH THE CALIFORNIA INSURANCE CODE. (SEE INS. CODE SECTION 10291.5.) THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES HE FAILED TO PERFORM THE DUTIES IMPOSED UPON HIM. THE COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MANDAMUS. =(302/CWW)
